exercises.title: ALL CAPS ALERT
exercises.prompt:
Define **yell(msg)** that returns `msg` converted to all uppercase letters.
~~~lua
yell("breach")  -- "BREACH"
~~~
Use the **string.upper** function (or the method form `msg:upper()`).
exercises.starter:
function yell(msg)
  -- TODO: return msg in uppercase
  return msg
end
exercises.lore: When the grid screams, it screams in capitals.

exercises.title: KEY LENGTH
exercises.prompt:
Define **key_length(key)** that returns the number of characters in the string `key`.
~~~lua
key_length("abcd")  -- 4
~~~
Use the **#** length operator on the string.
exercises.starter:
function key_length(key)
  -- TODO: return the length of key
  return 0
end
exercises.lore: A key too short opens nothing.

exercises.title: FIRST FRAGMENT
exercises.brief: Slice the head off a string.
exercises.prompt:
Define **first_n(text, n)** that returns the first `n` characters of `text`.
~~~lua
first_n("hovercraft", 5)  -- "hover"
~~~
Use **string.sub(text, 1, n)** (Lua strings are 1-indexed).
exercises.starter:
function first_n(text, n)
  -- TODO: return the first n characters
  return ""
end

tests.name: n of one returns one char

exercises.lore: The first fragment tells you what kind of file you stole.

exercises.title: VOWEL SWEEP
exercises.brief: Walk a string and tally the vowels.
exercises.prompt:
Define **count_vowels(text)** that returns how many lowercase vowels (`a`, `e`, `i`, `o`, `u`) appear in `text`. Walk the characters by index from `1` to `#text`, pull each one with **string.sub(text, i, i)**, and add to a counter when it is a vowel.
~~~lua
count_vowels("matrix")  -- 2
~~~
Assume `text` is already lowercase.
exercises.starter:
function count_vowels(text)
  local total = 0
  -- TODO: walk each char and count vowels
  return total
end
exercises.solution:
function count_vowels(text)
  local total = 0
  for i = 1, #text do
    local c = string.sub(text, i, i)
    if c == "a" or c == "e" or c == "i" or c == "o" or c == "u" then
      total = total + 1
    end
  end
  return total
end
exercises.hint: loop i = 1, #text and test string.sub(text, i, i) against each vowel
exercises.lore: Strip the vowels and the message still breathes. Barely.

exercises.title: CAPITALIZE NODE
exercises.brief: Uppercase the first letter, lowercase the rest.
exercises.prompt:
Define **capitalize(word)** that returns `word` with its first character uppercased and every following character lowercased.
~~~lua
capitalize("nEBuchadnezzar")  -- "Nebuchadnezzar"
~~~
Use **string.sub** to split off the first character from the rest, then **string.upper** and **string.lower** on the pieces and join them with **..**. Return `""` unchanged when `word` is empty.
exercises.starter:
function capitalize(word)
  -- TODO: uppercase first char, lowercase the rest
  return word
end
exercises.solution:
function capitalize(word)
  if #word == 0 then
    return ""
  end
  local head = string.upper(string.sub(word, 1, 1))
  local tail = string.lower(string.sub(word, 2))
  return head .. tail
end
exercises.hint: head = string.upper(string.sub(word,1,1)); tail = string.lower(string.sub(word,2)); return head .. tail
exercises.lore: Even a ship's name deserves to be spelled with pride.
